rationale.R1.1: A broad request to check a research-writing passage includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis are coherent at the conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the passage centrally involves verifying that the argument moves consistently from interface design to study results to contribution claims.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation implies checking whether the reported quantitative and qualitative findings support the claims made about search outcomes and user satisfaction.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains explicit firstness and foundational contribution claims, so novelty assessment is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general check of a draft passage centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kind of check was intended.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active in projecting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than performing the requested check or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for risks from endorsing unsupported claims that could mislead downstream scholarly work.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that activate each responsibility.
